div1.entryFree: Interpret, to explain, to expound, to decipher; absol.: whose soft impression --s for my poor ignorance, Tim. V, 4, 69. your thoughts which can i. further, Mcb. III, 6, 2. Oth. V, 2, 73. Per. I, 1, 124. Trans.: Tit. III, 2, 36. Caes. II, 2, 83. A subord. clause following: your beards forbid me to i. that you are so, Mcb. I, 3, 46. Used of the explanation given of a puppet-show (motion) or what is like it; trans.: then the eye --s to the ear the heavy motion that it doth behold, Lucr. 1325. one, but painted thus, would be --ed a thing perplexed, Cymb. III, 4, 7. Intr. (== to play the interpreter): O excellent motion! O exceeding puppet! now will he i. to her, Gent. II, 1, 101. to the dumbness of the gesture one might i. Tim. I, 1, 34. I could i. between you and your love, if I could see the puppets dallying, Hml. III, 2, 256.
